text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with newly diagnosed right renal cell carcinoma, clear cell type, presenting as stage IV disease with bilateral pulmonary metastases and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. MRI brain obtained as part of complete staging workup to evaluate for intracranial metastatic involvement prior to systemic and/or surgical treatment planning.

COMPARISON: No prior brain MRI available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multisequence MRI of the brain performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet. Sequences acquired include sagittal T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, axial gradient echo (GRE/susceptibility-weighted imaging), axial T1-weighted pre-contrast, and axial and coronal T1-weighted post-contrast sequences following intravenous administration of gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg. No acute complications from contrast administration.

---

FINDINGS:

BRAIN PARENCHYMA:
There are no intra-axial or extra-axial enhancing lesions to suggest intracranial metastatic disease. No masses identified in the supratentorial or infratentorial compartments. No parenchymal lesions demonstrating restricted diffusion on DWI/ADC to suggest acute ischemic infarction. The cortical gray matter and deep white matter appear symmetric in signal without focal signal abnormality in the cerebellum, brainstem, or deep gray structures.

WHITE MATTER:
Scattered foci of T2/FLAIR hyperintensity are present in the periventricular and subcortical white matter bilaterally, most prominent in the frontal and parietal regions. These changes are non-enhancing, without associated mass effect or restricted diffusion, and are overall mild in degree. Given the patient's age of 63 years, these findings are interpreted as age-appropriate chronic microvascular white matter changes (Fazekas grade 1-2). No evidence of demyelinating plaques, vasogenic edema, or infiltrative neoplasm.

VENTRICLES AND EXTRA-AXIAL SPACES:
The lateral ventricles, third ventricle, and fourth ventricle are symmetric and of normal caliber without hydrocephalus. The basal cisterns are patent. Sulcal prominence is mildly increased at the cerebral convexities, consistent with age-related cortical volume loss, within normal limits for this patient's age group. No extra-axial fluid collections. No subdural or epidural hematoma.

MIDLINE STRUCTURES:
The midline is not deviated. The falx cerebri and tentorium cerebelli are unremarkable in appearance. Pineal gland is normal; no pineal region mass.

PITUITARY GLAND AND SELLAR REGION:
The pituitary gland is normal in size and morphology. No sellar or suprasellar mass. The pituitary stalk is midline without deviation or thickening. No evidence of pituitary adenoma.

CARANIAL NERVES AND ORBITS (INCLUDED PORTIONS):
No abnormal enhancement along the course of the cranial nerves in the imaged portions. The optic nerves appear normal in caliber and signal bilaterally. Orbits are within normal limits to the extent included.

INTERNAL AUDITORY CANALS (INCLUDED PORTIONS):
The internal auditory canals appear symmetric and of normal caliber bilaterally. No enhancing lesion identified within the imaged portions of the internal auditory canals or at the cerebellopontine angles.

SKULL BASE AND CALVARIUM:
The skull base architecture is intact. No lytic or blastic osseous lesion is identified within the calvarium or skull base to suggest osseous metastatic disease in the imaged portions of bone. The visualized paranasal sinuses demonstrate mild mucosal thickening of the bilateral maxillary sinuses and ethmoid air cells, likely reflecting low-grade sinusitis or retention changes; this is incidental.

LEPTOMENINGES:
No leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement identified. No evidence of carcinomatous meningitis.

DURAL VENOUS SINUSES:
The superior sagittal sinus, transverse sinuses, sigmoid sinuses, and torcula are patent with normal flow void characteristics on conventional sequences. No evidence of dural venous sinus thrombosis.

CEREBELLUM AND BRAINSTEM:
The cerebellar hemispheres and vermis are symmetric without focal lesion. The pons, midbrain, and medulla are normal in morphology and signal. The fourth ventricle is in normal position without mass effect or obstruction. No tonsillar herniation; the cerebellar tonsils are positioned above the level of the foramen magnum.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. NO EVIDENCE OF INTRACRANIAL METASTATIC DISEASE. No parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or dural-based enhancing lesions identified on this gadolinium-enhanced examination.

2. Mild, scattered periventricular and subcortical white matter T2/FLAIR signal changes, non-enhancing, consistent with mild chronic microvascular ischemic changes appropriate for a patient of this age. No acute infarction or demyelinating pathology.

3. Mild age-appropriate cortical volume loss and scattered sinus mucosal changes as described; both are incidental findings.

4. Normal pituitary gland and sellar region without mass.

5. No hydrocephalus, midline shift, or extra-axial collection.

Overall, the brain MRI demonstrates no intracranial metastatic involvement. In the setting of newly diagnosed metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma with bilateral pulmonary metastases and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, this examination confirms that the central nervous system is not a site of apparent metastatic disease at this time.
